description: A horizontal slogan graphic on a pale light-blue background that carries a faint watermark-style circular DHS/USCIS-type seal (the words 'DEPARTMENT' and 'OF' are partly legible around an eagle and Statue of Liberty motif). A large dark-navy serif headline at the upper left and center reads 'Green cards and visas are a privilege, not a right.', with the word 'privilege' set in italics and underlined by a hand-drawn red swoosh. Below the text are two specimen immigration documents shown at an angle: at left a sample 'UNITED STATES OF AMERICA PERMANENT RESIDENT' card bearing the placeholder name 'SPECIMEN / TEST V,' USCIS# 000-000-310, category IR1, country of birth Bahamas, date of birth 17 AUG 1958, with a generic male ID photo over a Statue of Liberty and flag background; at right a larger sample U.S. 'IMMIGRANT VISA' page issued by 'US CONSULATE GENERAL LONDON,' with placeholder holder name 'TRAVELER / HAPPYPERSON,' category IR1, and a machine-readable zone 'VIUSATRAVELER<<HAPPYPERSON<<<...' plus a generic female ID photo.
notable_text: Green cards and visas are a privilege, not a right.
